sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
So the right edge green (N) bar changed to white (W) on rows 0 and 1. The green bar seems to be a timer/progress bar that's shrinking. Each RIGHT press causes the green bar to shrink from the top. But the puzzle elements (top bar, left panel, right panel) didn't change on this second RIGHT press.
Wait, let me re-check. On the first RIGHT press, the blue/sky blue blocks on the right end of the top bar swapped. On the second RIGHT press, only the green bar changed. Let me look more carefully at the top bar.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check if the top bar right end changed on second RIGHT
before = last_transition.before_frame.ascii.split('\n')
after = last_transition.after_frame.ascii.split('\n')

for i in range(17, 23):
    b = before[i][35:45]
    a = after[i][35:45]
    if b != a:
        print(f"Row {i}: {b} -> {a}")
    else:
        print(f"Row {i}: {b} (no change)")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
OK so the second RIGHT only changed the green bar (timer). The top bar right end stayed as `SSbb` (which was the result of the first RIGHT press). So the green bar is a timer that shrinks with each action, and the top bar swap only happened on the first RIGHT press.
Wait, but maybe the top bar has multiple states. Let me think about this differently. Maybe:
1. The top bar has a blue block on the left and a pair of blocks (blue + sky blue) on the right
2. Pressing RIGHT moves the blue block from the left position to the right position (or vice versa)
3. The green bar is a timer
But the second RIGHT didn't change the top bar. Maybe it cycles through states: initial -> swapped -> something else?
Let me try LEFT to see if it reverses the swap.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Try LEFT to see if it reverses the swap
action(['LEFT'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
